more: n. m. S. पश्चात्, , Pālī pachchā . 1. The hinder part; the rear. Pīchhā pīchhā hī hai . Prov. The last the last remains. (No time like the present). 2. Following; pursuit; prosecution; persecution. pīchhā chhuṛā'nā , v. a. To rid oneself of; shake off; get rid of. Baṛī mushkil meṅ apnā pīchhā chhuṛāyā hai . 2. To release; liberate; remove any obstacle. Khudā ke wāste merā pīchhā chhuṛā do! pīchhā bhārī honā , v. n. To have the enemy in the rear. pīchhā chhoṛnā , v. n. To give up or over; drop; cease; let alone. pīchhā karnā, yā lenā , v. a. 1. To follow; go after; pursue; chase. 2. To press; importune; prosecute. Bhāgte kā pīchhā nahīṅ karte . 3. To recoil (a gun, etc.). pīchhā na chhoṛnā , v. n. To press eagerly; hang upon; pursue to the last; persecute.

Sāṅjh sunī piyā āvan piyārī, sundar nāri siṅgār banāī, Pīyar kesar, pīyar besar, pīyar hār līye larkāī, Pīyar chīr dīyo Kamlāpatī, pīyar chandan deh lagāī, Pīyar pān kī bīrī lagī, piyārī pīrī bhaī, pīū nahīṅ āī . Kabīt. Her husband would return at eve, the loved one heard, her toilette made, With paint, nose-ring, and garland round her neck, in golden hues arrayed, Golden her vesture too, and golden sandal on her body shone, Ripe yellow pān she chewed—her spouse not come, the maid was yellow grown. pīlā-pan, pilāī , n. f. Yellowness ( zardī ). pīle-bādal , W. n. m. Break of day; dawn. pīlī , n. f. A gold-mohur ( asharfī ).

more: Mah. n. m. 1. Monday (H. som-wār ). 2. A holy man (Mah); a saint; priest; spiritual guide; a head of a religious order. 1. Ek main dekhā aisā nar, pīr, bhaṭyārā, bhishtī, khar . Riddle. Ans. A Brahman. I saw a man who in himself combined a priest, cook, water-bearer, and an ass conjoined. 2. Pīr ko, na shahīd ko, pahle nakṭe deo ko! Prov. Nor saint nor martyr is served yet, this noseless monster offering first demands. 3. Ghar ke pīroṅ ko tel kā malīdā . Prov. For the house priest only oil cakes. 4. Baṛheṅ to amīr, ghaṭeṅ to faqīr, mareṅ to pīr! Prov. Who thrive are nobles, who decline are holy ascetics, who die are saints. (The proverb is used by Hindus to indicate the astute policy of the Mahomedans who have rewards for every condition in life). 3. (Ironic.) A consummate knave. pīr karnā , v. n. To adopt a teacher or spiritual guide. Pānī pīje chhān-ke, aur pīr kīje /?/ān-ke . Prov. Be…

def: v.n. To have pain; to be painful, to pain, ache.
more: E. v. n. H. pīṛā pain To feel any pain; to ache. 1. Bhāt khāte hāth pirāë . Prov. She cant eat rice— it makes her hand ache? (She is so delicate!) 2. Sakṛī kuīyāṅ, patāl kheṛe pānī, Bharat bharat morī kamar pirānī . Song. Ṭhumrī . A narrow well, the water deep as hell, My sides ache sore from drawing at the well. 3. Mār! muë, mār! terī hatṛī pir/?/ë, merī ādat na jāe . Beat, beat me, wretch! your hands will pain, my ways I'll not give up. (The wife to the husband).
v. n. To have pain, be painful, pain, ache, ake.

more: n. f. 1. Old age; senility. 1. Waqt pīrī, shabāb kī bāteṅ, Aisī haiṅ jaise khuāb kī bāteṅ . Zauq. The joys of youth to old age are Like dreams that vanish in thin air. 2. Munāsib yeh pīrī meṅ gaflat nahīṅ; Uṭho, sone-wālo, sahar ho gaī! Mīr. This trifling in old age's not meet, Sleeper awake! the morning greet! 3. Jawānī gaī, ā gayā waqt pīrī; Kabhī tīr the, ab kamāṅ ho gaë haiṅ . His youth all spent, old age now on him sent, Foretime as arrow straight, a bow now bent. 2. The office of a priest or saint. 3. Rule; authority; power; sway; influence. 1. Aisī hī tumhāre bāvā kī pīrī hai? Has your father any authority here? (Said to an intruder or oppressor). 2. Bigaṛne meṅ bhī zulf uskī banā kī, Na kuchh pīrī chalī bād-i-sabā kī . Momin. Even tossed and blown her hair all lovely shown, In vain the envious winds 'gainst her are borne.
n. f. 1. Stubble from which a second crop is obtained (E. doṅjī ). 2. The trunk of a tree. 3. A rate paid by cultivators to zamindars for the use of fruit trees.
